title: Podman /dev mount can't be manually set with --tmpfs
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Host /dev is mounted as:

```
none on /dev type devtmpfs (rw,nosuid,noexec,relatime,size=15932176k,nr_inodes=3983044,mode=755)
```

Container by default gets `/dev` mounted as, according to the config.json: 

```
    {
      "destination": "/dev",
      "type": "tmpfs",
      "source": "tmpfs",
      "options": [
        "nosuid",
        "strictatime",
        "mode=755",
        "size=65536k"
      ]
    },
```

I am trying to mount my container `/dev` manually so I can add additional mount options.
**However**, when I try and mount it manually with the **default mount options** it fails to launch the container in interactive psudeo terminal mode. 

**2 Questions:**

1. Why is this not working? 
2. Why is `/dev` not matching my hosts `noexec` 

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1.
```
sudo podman --storage-driver=vfs create --read-only -it --tmpfs=/dev:nosuid,strictatime,mode=755,size=65536k --name test --rootfs /data/rootfs /bin/bash
```
2.
```
sudo podman --storage-driver=vfs start -ia test
```
3.
`
Error: unable to start container 6d9bd39dac12be88ef26e192b3e0eb2e37699b9f9334212edf1b563977c14930: container_linux.go:349: starting container process caused "process_linux.go:449: container init caused \"open /dev/ptmx: no such file or directory\"": OCI runtime attempted to invoke a command that was not found
`

IF I remove `--tmpfs=/dev:nosuid,strictatime,mode=755,size=65536k` it works and the mount options show:

`
        "nosuid",
        "strictatime",
        "mode=755",
        "size=65536k"
`

**Describe the results you received:**
Container fails to start in interactive mode. 
It will work if I remove the `-it` flags, but this is odd behavior. 

**Describe the results you expected:**
I would expect the container to start normally, even if I create the `--tmpfs /dev` myself. 

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 3.3.0-dev

```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
podman info --debug
host:
  arch: arm64
  buildahVersion: 1.21.1
  cgroupControllers:
  - cpuset
  - cpu
  - cpuacct
  - blkio
  - memory
  - devices
  - freezer
  - net_cls
  - perf_event
  - net_prio
  - hugetlb
  - pids
  - debug
  cgroupManager: cgroupfs
  cgroupVersion: v1
  conmon:
    package: 'conmon: /usr/local/bin/conmon'
    path: /usr/local/bin/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 2.0.29-dev, commit: '
  cpus: 8
  distribution:
    distribution: ubuntu
    version: "18.04"
  eventLogger: file
  hostname: pan-appliance
  idMappings:
    gidmap: null
    uidmap: null
  kernel: 4.9.140
  linkmode: dynamic
  memFree: 24989339648
  memTotal: 33424560128
  ociRuntime:
    name: runc
    package: 'runc: /usr/sbin/runc'
    path: /usr/sbin/runc
    version: 'runc version spec: 1.0.1-dev'
  os: linux
  remoteSocket:
    path: /run/podman/podman.sock
  security:
    apparmorEnabled: true
    capabilities: CAP_AUDIT_WRITE,CAP_CHOWN,CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE,CAP_FOWNER,CAP_FSETID,CAP_KILL,CAP_MKNOD,CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE,CAP_NET_RAW,CAP_SETFCAP,CAP_SETGID,CAP_SETPCAP,CAP_SETUID,CAP_SYS_CHROOT
    rootless: false
    seccompEnabled: true
    seccompProfilePath: /usr/share/containers/seccomp.json
    selinuxEnabled: false
  serviceIsRemote: false
  slirp4netns:
    executable: ""
    package: ""
    version: ""
  swapFree: 16712269824
  swapTotal: 16712269824
  uptime: 49h 50m 32.94s (Approximately 2.04 days)
registries:
  search:
  - docker.io
  - registry.fedoraproject.org
  - registry.access.redhat.com
store:

```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```

```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**


No

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
AWS

comments.body:
So I just tried to create the container, with `podman create`. 

Then I used `podman init` to create the config.json. 

I manually edited the config.json, to add `noexec` for the `/dev` mount. Then I started the container. 

However, it seems it to overwrite my overwrite, and remains default? 
Or maybe podman does not allow modifications of config.json after it has been initialized? 

When I type `mount` in the container I see:

`tmpfs on /dev type tmpfs (rw,nosuid,size=65536k,mode=755,uid=1004,gid=1006)`

Also I am using `uidmap` and `gidmap` flags when I create the container. 

So it did not work.

I would like to know if I have any options to set the `noexec` on this dev mount, and how I would do it? 
Or is this a bug?

I think it should be fine to add `noexec` to `/dev`.

We already have a `--security-opt proc-opts=...` to pass additional arguments for the `/proc` mount, I think we could have a similar option for `/dev` in case users want to opt out from the default.

@giuseppe that would be great! 


1. Do you know why setting the `--tmpfs` manually is not working? 

2. And do you know where the  `/dev` defaults are?  
Is this hardcoded somewhere?

>     1. Do you know why setting the `--tmpfs` manually is not working?

because you override the `/dev/pts` mount.

>     2. And do you know where the  `/dev` defaults are?
>        Is this hardcoded somewhere?

yes, they are hardcoded in `vendor/github.com/opencontainers/runtime-tools/generate/generate.go`

Thanks @giuseppe so I can simply use `--tmpfs /dev` AND `--mount=type=devtps,destination=/dev/pts/` and then I can override the `/dev` mount with my options.

Am I able to specifiy `noexec` when using the `--mount` approach?
Should we change the default, if this just works, and it would be more secure to mount /dev with noexec, we should just do it.  Although this is buries in runtime-tools.

Doesn't seem like '--mount' lets me specify 'noexec' for `/dev/pts`, from what I can tell. 
Unless I'm missing something @giuseppe and @rhatdan?

Hmm, when I do: 

`
--tmpfs=/dev:noexec,nosuid,strictatime,mode=755,size=65536k --mount=type=devpts,destination=/dev/pts
`

The `config.json` looks like this:

```
...
    {
      "destination": "/dev",
      "type": "tmpfs",
      "source": "tmpfs",
      "options": [
        "noexec",
        "nosuid",
        "strictatime",
        "mode=755",
        "size=65536k"
      ]
    },
    {
      "destination": "/dev/pts",
      "type": "devpts",
      "source": "devpts"
    },
...
```

And in the actual container, when I type: `mount`,
I see:

```
tmpfs on /dev type tmpfs (rw,nosuid,noexec,size=65536k,mode=755,uid=1004,gid=1006)
devpts on /dev/pts type devpts (rw,nosuid,noexec,relatime,gid=100004,mode=620,ptmxmode=666)
```

So this works for me! 

I am curious if those must be the default mount options for type: `devpts`
There does not appear to be "options" logic here:
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/a2acd04447a66027dc02a210d2961fcde6868985/pkg/specgenutil/volumes.go#L451

Seems I am hitting the default logic here:

https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/266a3892f25d8cee508f421e718ba6f135ff7123/pkg/specgen/generate/oci.go#L231-L254

And this works for my use case it seems.
